title: Moldova Symposium Report: Advocating to Let Libraries Be Libraries
content:
Blog post by Mariana Harjevschi

On 25 March 2026, Mariana Harjevschi, KR21 national coordinator for Moldova, presented the KR21 roadmap “Safeguard Access, Empower Europe: An Action Plan to Let Libraries be Libraries” at the 35th National Symposium “The Library Year 2025”, held in Chișinău. See the recording of Mariana’s intervention.

The symposium was organised by the National Library of the Republic of Moldova under the auspices of the Ministry of Culture. The theme of this year’s edition, “The Involvement of Libraries in Digital Transformation: Opportunities and Challenges”, reflects the strategic direction of 2025 and highlights the essential role of libraries in supporting the digital transforma

title: Different Players, Same Game: What Libraries Have in Common With Gamers
content:
The European Citizens’ Initiative (ECI) on the right to resell digital games is on the agenda of a joint hearing by the European Parliament’s Internal Market and Consumer Protection (IMCO), Legal Affairs (JURI), and Petitions (PETI) Committee on 16 April.

The ECI addresses a practice that many consumers know well: buying a game on the understanding that they own it, only to find it switched off or deleted by the company that sold it. Knowledge Rights 21 (KR21) supports the push for action, and we have a direct interest in its outcome, because the problem the ECI identifies is not specific to games.

Libraries Face the Same Structural Imbalance

Libraries and the researchers, educators, learners, and citizens who depend on them operate under conditions that are structurally identical to those the ECI describes. Licensed digital collections disappear without warning. Access is conditional, temporary, and governed by contract terms that no individual

title: European Rights Retention Community of Practice – Ninth Meeting
content:
When?

  • Date: 11 May 2026
  • Time: 12:00 CEST

What?

This Community of Practice is an online collaborative space designed to bring together professionals to discuss and tackle the challenges around rights retention strategies.

Save Your Spot!

Register now to secure your spot.

The Webinar

At this 9th meeting, Knowledge Rights 21 (KR21) partner SPARC Europe welcomes speakers from Ireland and the UK to share their experiences and perspectives:

  • Ireland: Susan Reilly – IReL (Irish Research e-Library)
  • UK: Anna Vernon – Jisc
